name: ROKU, INC
summary: Roku, Inc. is the leading TV streaming platform in the United States, Canada, and Mexico by hours streamed. The company operates the Roku TV OS, a purpose-built television operating system powering Roku streaming devices including streaming players, Roku-made TVs, and licensed Roku TV partner models. Roku connects viewers to content, enables content partners to monetize audiences, and provides advertisers with digital advertising capabilities. Revenue streams include platform advertising, content distribution, and hardware device sales.

products.name: Roku TV OS
products.description: Purpose-built operating system for TVs powering all Roku streaming devices and licensed Roku TV partner models.

products.name: The Roku Channel
products.description: Roku-owned streaming service aggregating AVOD, FAST, and Premium Subscriptions content.

products.name: Roku Streaming Players
products.description: HDMI-connected streaming devices including Roku Streaming Stick, Roku Ultra, and Roku Streambar SE.

products.name: Roku-made TVs
products.description: TVs designed, made, and sold by Roku including Roku Select, Roku Plus, Roku Pro Series, and Hiro Roku TVs.

products.name: Roku Home Screen
products.description: Primary user interface viewers see when streaming, used for content discovery and advertising monetization.

products.name: Roku Originals
products.description: Original content programming created by Roku for its streaming platform.

products.name: Supply-side Platform (SSP)
products.description: Programmatic advertising technology enabling publishers to sell ad inventory to multiple demand sources.

partnerships.description: TV manufacturers that license the Roku TV OS and use Roku smart TV reference designs to build Roku TV models.
Streaming app available on the Roku platform as a content partner.
Offers Premium Subscriptions through The Roku Channel as a content partner.
